title: GPT-5.5 Instant: OpenAI's Smarter, Less Hallucinatory Default ChatGPT Model
summary: OpenAI has updated ChatGPT's default model to GPT-5.5 Instant, delivering 52.5% fewer hallucinations and more concise, personalized responses for all users.
content: OpenAI has launched GPT-5.5 Instant as the new default model for all ChatGPT users, replacing GPT-5.3 Instant globally. Internal evaluations show the model produced 52.5% fewer hallucinated claims than its predecessor on high-stakes prompts covering medicine, law, and finance, and reduced inaccurate claims by 37.3% on conversations flagged for factual errors. Responses are designed to be tighter and more to-the-point, cutting back on verbosity, overformatting, and gratuitous emojis without losing substance. The model also introduces enhanced personalization by intelligently pulling context from users' past chats, saved files, and connected Gmail accounts, while giving users visibility and control over what context was used. For paid users, GPT-5.3 Instant remains available for three months before being retired.

title: Mistral Medium 3.5: 128B Open-Weight Model Built for Long-Horizon Agentic Engineering
summary: Mistral has released Medium 3.5, a 128B dense model that merges instruction-following, reasoning, and coding into a single set of weights, scoring 77.6% on SWE-Bench Verified.
content: Mistral's new flagship Medium 3.5 is a 128B dense model with a 256K context window, built for long-horizon agentic work by merging instruction-following, reasoning, and coding into a single set of weights. The model features configurable reasoning effort, allowing developers to dial up its depth for complex refactors or keep it lightweight for quick edits, and it scores 77.6% on SWE-Bench Verified, outperforming Devstral 2 and models like Qwen3.5 397B A17B. It can run self-hosted on as few as four GPUs and its vision encoder was trained from scratch to handle variable image sizes. Mistral released Medium 3.5 under a modified MIT license, staying true to its open-source principles, and it has become the new default for the Mistral Vibe CLI and Le Chat. API pricing is $1.50 per million input tokens and $7.50 per million output tokens, making it competitive for frontier-class agentic workloads.

title: Google, Microsoft, and xAI Agree to Pre-Release Government Safety Tests for AI Models
summary: All five major frontier AI labs have now agreed to let the US Department of Commerce evaluate their models before public release, catalyzed by concerns over Anthropic's powerful Mythos model.
content: The US Department of Commerce's Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI) announced voluntary pre-deployment testing agreements with Google DeepMind, Microsoft, and xAI, joining existing partners OpenAI and Anthropic to give the government early access to evaluate all five major frontier AI labs' models. The move was catalyzed by Anthropic's Mythos model, which the company says is 'far ahead' of other models in cybersecurity capabilities and has been restricted to select approved organizations due to its ability to identify and exploit software vulnerabilities. CAISI, which has already completed over 40 AI model evaluations including unreleased state-of-the-art models, will assess new AI systems for national security, cybersecurity, biosecurity, and chemical weapons risks prior to release. The agreements are voluntary and give CAISI no authority to block a release, making them goodwill commitments that companies view as preferable to the risk of mandatory legislation. The White House is simultaneously weighing a formal executive order that could transform this voluntary system into mandatory pre-deployment approval.

title: White House Weighs Executive Order for Mandatory AI Model Vetting Before Public Release
summary: The Trump administration is considering a 16-page executive order that would require AI companies to obtain government approval before releasing advanced models, marking a sharp reversal from its hands-off approach.
content: The Trump administration is actively deliberating over an executive order that would establish a mandatory government vetting regime requiring AI companies to receive approval before releasing advanced models, according to seven industry representatives and policy advisers. A 16-page draft order circulating within the White House would prohibit private-sector companies from 'interfering' with the government's use of AI models and introduce more aggressive contracting and termination standards for federal AI vendors. The deliberations represent a significant policy reversal for an administration that had previously favored deregulation of AI at the urging of venture capitalists like David Sacks and Marc Andreessen. The primary driver is Anthropic's Mythos model, which can identify and exploit software vulnerabilities faster than human hackers and has alarmed senior officials including White House Chief of Staff Susie Wiles and Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent. The White House is also reportedly working to review the Pentagon's supply chain risk designation against Anthropic, which has spawned two active lawsuits.

title: IBM Think 2026: Watsonx Orchestrate Evolves into Multi-Agent Control Plane as AI Divide Widens
summary: IBM unveiled its most comprehensive enterprise AI expansion at Think 2026, including a next-gen watsonx Orchestrate, the IBM Concert operations platform, and the generally available IBM Sovereign Core.
content: At its annual Think 2026 conference in Boston, IBM announced the next generation of watsonx Orchestrate as an agentic control plane for the multi-agent era, allowing organizations to deploy AI agents from any source with consistent policy enforcement and real-time accountability. IBM simultaneously launched IBM Concert platform, an AI-powered operations system that correlates signals across applications, infrastructure, and networks into a single unified view to move enterprises from passive monitoring to coordinated, intelligent response. The company made IBM Sovereign Core generally available—a platform embedding governance and compliance at the infrastructure runtime level for regulated industries and cross-border deployments, supported by ecosystem partners including AMD, Cloudera, Dell, Intel, and Mistral. Leveraging its recent acquisition of Confluent, IBM is delivering real-time data streaming to AI agents, with a Nestlé proof of concept demonstrating 83% cost savings and a 30x price-performance improvement. IBM framed the announcements around a widening 'AI divide,' arguing that enterprises pulling ahead are redesigning how they operate rather than simply deploying more AI tools.

title: Tableau Transforms into Agentic Analytics Platform, Enabling AI to Act on Data Autonomously
summary: Salesforce's Tableau has reimagined itself as a knowledge and decision engine for the agentic era, allowing AI agents to not just surface insights but take autonomous action across enterprise systems.
content: Tableau unveiled its Agentic Analytics Platform on May 5, transforming from a data visualization tool into a high-scale knowledge and decision engine that enables AI agents to take autonomous, trusted action across enterprise systems. The platform introduces headless analytics via an open MCP server architecture, delivering trusted insights directly into Slack, Salesforce, Microsoft Teams, Claude, ChatGPT, and other tools where teams already work—without requiring users to log into Tableau. A new decision engine closes the loop between analysis and action by automatically triggering workflows such as creating Salesforce cases, routing alerts to team leads, or launching remediation workflows based on data signals. The Agentic Analytics Command Center provides centralized governance and observability over all running agents and their data access, with general availability coming in Fall 2026. New integrations with Microsoft Teams, Slack, and Google Workspace are live today; the platform is already trusted by 97% of the Fortune 100.
